answer: To publish a GitHub Pages site privately, you need to have an organization account. Additionally, your organization must use GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

GitHub Free for personal accounts: 2,000 minutes per month  
GitHub Pro: 3,000 minutes per month  
GitHub Enterprise Cloud: 50,000 minutes per month  
Difference between highest and lowest values: 48,000 minutes

Certain contributor, commit, and code frequency insights are only available for repositories that have fewer than 10,000 commits. This constraint affects GitHub Pro, GitHub Team, and GitHub Enterprise plans.
Any usage beyond the included amounts is billed to your account. GitHub Actions usage is free for standard GitHub-hosted runners in public repositories, and for self-hosted runners.

GitHub Free for personal accounts includes two-factor authentication enforcement. Additional security features available exclusively in GitHub Enterprise include authentication with SAML single sign-on, access provisioning with SAML or SCIM, additional security, compliance, and deployment controls, deployment protection rules with GitHub Actions for private or internal repositories, audit log streaming, IP allow list, and the option to host your company's data in a specific region, on a unique subdomain.
